scenarios.title: iPhone Operated Outside Intended Use
scenarios.description: The iPhone suffered motherboard failure after being used repeatedly as a dashcam for several months in an automobile exposed to extreme heat and sunlight.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to damage caused by operating the Covered iPhone outside the permitted or intended uses described by the manufacturer.
ADH coverage only applies to operational or mechanical failure caused by an unexpected and unintentional external event arising from normal daily usage of the Covered iPhone as intended.

scenarios.reasoning: The device failure resulted from prolonged use as a dashcam, leading to exposure beyond environmental operating guidelines. The policy specifically excludes damage resulting from use outside manufacturer-intended purposes (section 4.2(ii)(c)), and accidental damage coverage only applies to unintended external events arising from normal daily use (section 3.2). Service fees and ADH limits do not apply because this claim is not eligible.

scenarios.title: Refused Claim for iPhone with Removed Serial Number
scenarios.description: Customer requests hardware service on an iPhone, but investigation reveals the serial number has been physically removed from the device.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to an iPhone with a serial number that has been altered, defaced or removed, or has been modified to alter its functionality or capability without the written permission of the manufacturer.

scenarios.reasoning: Hardware and ADH coverage are void when the device serial number has been removed, per section 4.2(iii). Since Apple cannot verify eligibility or authenticity, no repair or replacement is authorized and no service fee applies.
